text.content:
In the forestry industry, downtime is a directly impacts productivity and profit.

A single tire failure can stall operations, cost thousands in repairs, and jeopardize crew safety. At MAXAM, we understand how tire maintenance is critical for day-to-day operations. Below, we jump into a detailed guide on the four crucial checks every operator should perform before starting a shift, ensuring your skidder tires deliver maximum uptime and performance.

In the forestry industry, downtime is a directly impacts productivity and profit.

A single tire failure can stall operations, cost thousands in repairs, and jeopardize crew safety. At MAXAM, we understand how tire maintenance is critical for day-to-day operations. Below, we jump into a detailed guide on the four crucial checks every operator should perform before starting a shift, ensuring your skidder tires deliver maximum uptime and performance.

text.content: 1. Tire Pressure: Avoid Deflating Your Day-To-Day

rich_text.plain_text: 1. Tire Pressure: Avoid Deflating Your Day-To-Day

text.content: 4. Valve Caps and Lug Nuts: Small Parts, Big Impact

rich_text.plain_text: 4. Valve Caps and Lug Nuts: Small Parts, Big Impact

text.content: Valve caps are your first defense against air loss. Check for cracks, corrosion, or leaks by spraying soapy water on the valve stem—bubbles signal trouble. Replace damaged caps immediately to maintain a secure seal.

rich_text.plain_text: Valve caps are your first defense against air loss. Check for cracks, corrosion, or leaks by spraying soapy water on the valve stem—bubbles signal trouble. Replace damaged caps immediately to maintain a secure seal.

text.content: Lug nuts/bolts are equally vital. Loose hardware can lead to wheel separation, a deadly hazard. Use a calibrated torque wrench to tighten lugs to the manufacturer’s specifications and recheck them after the first 50 hours of operation. Even minor vibration can loosen even properly tightened nuts.

rich_text.plain_text: Lug nuts/bolts are equally vital. Loose hardware can lead to wheel separation, a deadly hazard. Use a calibrated torque wrench to tighten lugs to the manufacturer’s specifications and recheck them after the first 50 hours of operation. Even minor vibration can loosen even properly tightened nuts.
